dialogueLines.text: I found her, Father. And I'm going to find Mother again. And again, and again, until I have some answers, finally. She'd like some answers, too, beginning with why did you never tell her that I lived? You let her go, and left her to her grief!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Silence, boy! Save all your ignorant and baseless accusations. You seem incapable of heeding my request that you not blather on about a matter you shall never understand. Then, I shall save my breath for this fight!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You were never supposed to live. Took all of Nyx's strength to circumvent what should have been a certainty. Now you cannot stay dead. Such is the wry humor of the Fates.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx saved me.... So according to the Fates I was never meant to be born, since neither you nor Mother are of the Underworld, something like that? Or was that just another lie you told, to Mother and whoever else?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Bah! You think everything a lie. The realm beneath our feet was appointed to me on certain conditions, not that I had much of a choice. Among them: A one-time surface-dweller such as I would never have an heir.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You really believed that? Then why attempt to have a child, anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You don't know anything! Why anyone would choose to have a child is a mystery to me. The Fates said I would never have an heir. Perhaps this was all they meant.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: To think Olympus has been aiding you. You must be so grateful to them for their aid, without which none of this would have been possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, about that. Father, did... did you let Mother go because you feared Olympus would find out? And... not respond well to the circumstances if they did?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Tsch. Be grateful they have not found out as yet, for all your meddling. You do not know them as well as you think. Ask yourself why she left Olympus, and how. Or better yet, ask her.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, she seems quite lovely, so... knowing you, you probably tricked her into signing a pact to come join you, or something. Since you weren't allowed on Olympus, why not have Olympus come to you?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Surprisingly not distant from the truth. I took her, boy! I took her down with me. Is that what you wish to hear? Does that anger you? Come, bring forth that anger, then!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: So, boy. Did you ask her? About how she came to join me in the Underworld. Did you confirm all your suspicions, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You didn't drag her down to hell, yourself, apparently. You're not going to tell me who did, though I can well imagine. Your brothers got to reign forever on Olympus, while you got sent down into the Underworld. So they gave you a consolation prize... didn't they?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You make such accusations, knowing next to nothing. Careless as ever. Poseidon had nothing to do with it! He knows even less than you. If you value her existence or your own, you would be wise to keep it that way. And to turn back!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...She was the Queen. Queen of the Underworld! My Queen. Commanding reverence, and awe, and fear. She was not just... what was the term you used? A consolation prize? You are as disrespectful to her as you are to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, what would you even know?! You claim so much respect for her, yet you didn't even respect her enough to tell her what happened after she left! But... what am I saying... I know you love her, still, Father. That best explains all the terrible choices you've made.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...All the terrible choices I've made. By the time you have existed for as long as I have, pray you will have made fewer. Although I fear you shall surpass me in at least this one regard.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...I have done virtually everything within my power to prevent this. All of it... for nothing. The Fates were not content to saddle me within the earth; they mock me, still.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Maybe they just think you're in the wrong. And that you're an obstinate, overbearing, miserable excuse for a husband and father. Don't you understand I'm trying to fix the problems you caused? Since you're so unwilling to do it yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You're right. I was unwilling. I made my choices, in all this. I loved her, yes. I loved her terribly. But now... it seems I am merely delaying the inevitable. Go, then. Go to her. But, should harm befall her... I shall bring my worst wrath upon whomever is to blame... including you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What? You're letting me go, just like that? You're just afraid of getting struck down again. You're serious?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...Blast you, boy... we should have never sprung from Chaos... none of this...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I... how... you.... Go. Go, then. Go!! Get out of here! Get out!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I told you that I would, Father. And I'm going to. Goodbye.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...Wait. Wait, just... if you... if you find her. If you somehow find her? Tell her something for me, tell her... Tell her Cerberus is doing very well.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What? Wait. I don't think I owe you any favors, here. Though, answer me one question, and I'll give it some thought, how's that?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...I know your question, boy. But, ask it anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Persephone. My mother. Why did she leave?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...I... urgh... blood and darkness...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're not going to cooperate with me on this, are you? Even now, knowing Mother wants to see me again?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Urgh! Blast you... you shall ruin everything... with all your damnable questions!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Then, die, already. And get the hell out of my way.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...I never grew accustomed to the air, up here. It gusts senselessly whichever way it pleases. I suppose you must prefer it to the stillness of the air below.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, you're right. The air up here seems pretty good so far. But I will reserve judgment till I've had my fill. I have to say, though, Father: The Helm of Darkness suits you. I'm touched you'd dust it off on my account.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: It should not have had to come to this. For all your antics with me and around the House, I always kept my temper, have I not? Unlike you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, let's see. You've berated me repeatedly and often. You've lied to me. Mustered your wretched forces to kill me, over and over. But, sure, I'll grant you that you've always kept most of your anger bottled up. All that's about to change, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: We're gods, boy. Killing one another is our lot. Although, I always thought I was above it. I told you that you cannot leave this place. It seems I must enforce my rules myself. I'm sending you home, now.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: No. You're going home, not me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...I never grew accustomed to the air, up here. It gusts senselessly whichever way it pleases. I suppose you must prefer it to the stillness of the air below.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, you're right. The air up here seems pretty good so far. But I will reserve judgment till I've had my fill. I have to say, though, Father: The Helm of Darkness suits you. I'm touched you'd dust it off on my account.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: It should not have had to come to this. For all your antics with me and around the House, I always kept my temper, have I not? Unlike you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well let's see. You've berated me repeatedly and often. You've lied to me. Mustered a bunch of incompetent wretches to try to kill me. But, sure, I'll grant you that you've always kept most of your anger bottled up. All that's about to change, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: We're gods, boy. Killing one another is our lot. Although, I always thought I was above it. I told you that you cannot leave this place. It seems I must enforce my rules myself. I'm sending you home, now.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: No. You're going home, not me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Why? Why do you keep showing up? In spite of knowing that the outcome shall be just the same as how it always was?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Beats having to toil away under your overbearing supervision. And besides, as you may be aware, I've recently made up my mind to leave this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Eughh... utterly incorrigible. When next we meet here, then, we'll keep it brief. I have grown weary of our aimless conversations. Now prepare!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You know now what you're doing here is without any purpose whatsoever. Even if I don't slay you myself, you'll simply perish when you cross that threshold there. And yet you still persist?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I do. Besides, making these treks helps pass the time during the ongoing Underworld renovations. And I'll be in tiptop shape once they're complete!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Would that we had a little more support; the renovations could have been complete by now! As ever, you think only of yourself. But if you must insist like this, then you will find me waiting for you once you get here. Every single time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I was reviewing the terms of the Pact of Punishment to which you are bound. Even when my servants resort to extreme measures, they were still unable to surpass you, then. So now, it is my turn.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Caught you unprepared, did I? After Theseus and his Macedonian, I half-expected to see you thundering around on some golden chariot of your own, drawn by sable steeds, that sort of thing. But it's just you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: It is just me, indeed. However... that should be more than necessary, here. No holding back this time, Zagreus. Are you ready?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Extreme measures permitted, even still? You must have forgotten to change the conditions of your Pact of Punishment.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wasn't an oversight. You're strong, Father. I want to be as strong, and more. Not that I'm taking after you, or anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You wish to test yourself against the full extent of my wrath, then very well. You have earned that right. Besides yourself, only the Titans have. Now, then... attack!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You vanquished me... even when I threw everything I had at you, and more. I fought you as I fought the Titans themselves, and still I failed.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, don't take it so hard, Father. There's always next time, right? Meaning, this time. Maybe I won't be so fortunate.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Of that, I am most certain. Come, let us see the limits of our abilities, then. I warn you: I shall hold nothing back. To the death!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Urgh.... <Gasp> How... I... I went all out! And still...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Urgh... no... I'm... not... finished... with you... yet!!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You bear the Sigil of the Dead. I wear the Helm of Darkness. Even devoid of artifacts as these, we fight here without witnesses... in shadow.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: If only everybody knew you had a mighty son most capable of cutting you down to size, with or without the ability to vanish suddenly.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: A very boastful son, who often thinks too much of his ability. Come, then! I'll make you vanish suddenly.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I never had any respect for Stygius, that blade you bear. Perhaps unfairly, due to Brother Poseidon's sloppy form when once he wielded it. But now it seems quite different in your care.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I think we've grown quite close to one another, yes. Enough to where I've come to know a different side of this fine blade right here. This is the aspect of some Arthur fellow! Maybe the two of you could get acquainted properly?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Arthur! No name either of gods or kings of any realm I know. Then, introduce us, as you say! To this new amateur, to whom your blade rendered its services.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: There you are, bearing my brother's blasted shield... flaunting it about so recklessly. Though, what have you done to it, now?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Not much, really, besides awakening the aspect of a supposedly unstoppable, dragon-slaying warrior called Beowulf. If it has the power to protect him against a giant lizard, I figure it may help me against you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You presently shall find I'm no mere giant lizard, boy. Though we shall see whether this Beowulf is any help to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Resorting to mere fisticuffs with me... though, how you got Malphon to look like that, I've no idea. Pray tell, whose monstrous claws are those? A manticore's?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: These claws, I'll have you know, Father, belonged once to an ancient god-king known as Gilgamesh. You ever meet?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Hrm. Provided he was flesh and blood at some point, then he resides within my realm. Presumably within Elysium somewhere, sadly bereft of his manticore claws, thanks to you. Come, show me what they can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: What have you done with my ancient weapon, boy? I sense Varatha's form there in your feeble grip, but... there's some garish power you've awakened in it, haven't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, just took a splash of Titan Blood at the right time was all. There's more to your ancient weapon than you gave it credit for, I guess. Though let's not keep it waiting, no?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: No, let's not. Gigaros is the mightier weapon, with none of the treacherous past. It needs no fanciful shape-shifting tricks to run you through. Behold!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You approach me with that loathsome Rail, boy. Emanating a hellish fury more even than usual, at that. Little surprise you took a shine to it. It was the weapon that I hated most of all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, someone called Lucifer apparently thought differently, and took a crack at his own father with it, once. This one's for him, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Invoking names you know nothing about. Presuming, all the while, far too much. You take that monstrous artifact for another friend? You would be wiser to be wary of its influence. But, let us fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: The bow is the weapon of a coward, boy. I said so even to Hera's face. I'm unsurprised that you are drawn to it, and yet... something has changed about it, I can tell.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean that Coronacht has taken on the aspect of a divine preserver person of some sort, of whom I've seen mere glimpses but seems definitely very capable, and really rather brave?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I know not why the old Infernal Arms endear themselves to you. And, I entirely care not in whose weakling aspect you bring them forth, only to fall to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Varatha... I recognize its presence, now. That's not just any three-pronged lance you wield. I could never stand to look at it, once my brothers and my sisters and myself... once our foul work was done.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You mean once you'd chopped up your cruel and overbearing Titan parents, right? Shame you threw it out. It's quite useful.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps the Fates decided that its wielders ought to all eventually wind up on its receiving end. Come, then! Let us see what the great Achilles has taught you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: So! You think that you can vanquish me with my fool brother's old, forgotten blade. Poseidon was never any good with Stygius. Grew envious of my spearwork. Now he's famous for that fork of his! Always taking credit where none is due.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Must be hard, knowing that everybody's going to think you learned your spearwork from Poseidon, not the other way around. As for Stygius, I can assure you it's in good hands now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Your assurances mean nothing to me, boy. You think yourself quite capable? Then show me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You come here bearing brother Zeus' Aegis. I wonder how much more insulting you could be.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What? I haven't even started to insult you here yet, Father. Flustered by the old Shield of Chaos, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Doubtless Zeus does not even remember wielding it. I never should have relied on him for anything after that! Then, come. Let's see if you're any better with it than your great uncle.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Look at yourself, boy. You barely stand, and yet believe you can surpass me in your current state?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's not exactly easy getting here, you know. But I'm not going back. Not yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps not yet. But very, very soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Blast you, boy. You think you are superior to me? You are a fool.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Urgh...! With your youth comes a certain strength, it seems.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Tsch... I... I shall see you back at home.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: How? How is this possible, that this... urgh!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Ungh... The world you seek out there... it's even crueler than the one you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Augh! Why... I was much stronger once, than this...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Aah, blast! That you of all my kin would be the one, to do this, to me....

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Mmph... No matter how many times you beat me... we're family. There's no escaping that.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Again, you have defeated me, somehow. And yet...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: What shall it take... what shall it take for you to...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Ungh, you... tell no one at the House. Do you hear me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Rrngh, you stubborn... you shrug off death as though it means nothing to you...!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...I shall keep coming back... the same as you...!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You... you think that you're the only one... who shall keep fighting?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I must admit... you've learned something. At some point, in all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Urgh... were it not for all my wretched kin upon Olympus, you...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Rngh, vanquished again... by my own fool kin....

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Guh! Boy, you speak none of this, to anyone!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Urgh! How did... you accomplish this...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Nrgh! You cannot escape... none of us can.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Augh... then, go, see what awaits you in this realm...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Gah... I, fell to you, again, I... how...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...The sea... the heavens, and the earth. All of this belongs to my brothers. We are their kin by birth. But we are foreigners in their country.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No. No mournful speeches. Now get out of my way.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You impertinent brat. Fine. Impulsiveness runs in the family.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: There was a time when Cerberus would never have permitted any soul to exit through that gateway there behind you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that can't possibly be true! What about Orpheus, Theseus, Heracles, Odysseus? Countless tales of brave men delving into the Underworld, then leaving whence they came!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: They left on my authority alone. Nor did they take for granted my good graces. You believe you are entitled simply to walk out of here. From your birthright and your responsibilities! But I am here to tell you, no.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: This cold white substance falling slowly from the heavens. Snow, they call it. Look carefully upon each fleck. Tiny crystals! But structurally weak. They melt! This surface realm... it is held up by mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: If you're quite finished teaching me about this snow phenomenon, can we get on with brutally trying to kill each other, here?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You wish to have your mortal-colored blood spill once again upon this bed of snow, then fine. Let us proceed.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: My brothers and my sisters and myself. We, too, wanted to slay our parents. The Titans. Bastards. And worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And you succeeded. Chopped up some of them into many tiny bits, and sprinkled them throughout the pits of Tartarus so that they can't regenerate! Or so I hear. Inspiring, really!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: We had no recourse at the time, save to collaborate. Developed a specific plan, and saw it through. And, the Titans...? You think me cruel, yet know nothing of cruelty. But, here, you want something to hate, then have it!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Ah, but there you are, boy, finally. Despite my knowing you are always late for everything, here you've outdone yourself by quite some time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh shut up, and get your fork, and burn away that regal cape, and let's get to this, then. I'd no idea you had all these capes to spare! Must have a whole entire storage chamber full of them.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Your mockery of me may temporarily embolden you, but achieves nothing useful, in the end.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You still have yet to see the sun itself. You know only the light of Ixion, but the sun? It is positively blinding. Hideous.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It has been snowy nightfall every single time now that you mention it. I didn't know it snowed so much up here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: It hasn't always. But, that's of no consequence to us. Especially when there's a raging battle to be fought.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I must admit to something, boy. Achilles taught you reasonably well. I expected that if anyone could train you into martial competence, it would be him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Though you didn't expect that, one day, I would end up using everything I learned from him against you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I anticipate more than you know. But Achilles merely practiced against mortals. Now, you practice against me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Gah... you have your mother's perseverance. How is it that you got this strong?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Urngh! It seems... I was no match for you, this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Augh... I'm not as limber as I used to be, while you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Nngh... for a student of Achilles... I fear that you surpass even his skill, by now...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Umphh... you broke through every one of my defenses, once again...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Argh... you bested me, I'll grant you, Zagreus...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Ungh... Olympus gave you quite the edge against me, then...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Grngh... you owe this victory to all your relatives...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Come all this way again, to fall to me? Would that you paid me such respects back at the House.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: What is it going to take for you to understand that your place is in the House?!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I shall give you no quarter, boy. Although I doubt that you expected otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: The sea and air are very still this eve. Though I suppose that is about to change.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Know only that I take no pleasure in what I am now forced to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: There you are. Then let us now commence this ugly business yet again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: The heavens and the seas belong to my brothers; but know that you are still in my domain.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You bypassed Cerberus again, I see. Unfortunately for the both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I had other pressing business to attend to. But I cannot let you leave.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: No matter how many times you try, your stubbornness shall get you nowhere with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You're back, with Stygius in hand. Come then, and show me your technique!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: The Blade of the Underworld cannot help you here, you realize that, boy?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Your heart-seeking Coronacht may have brought you this far, but shall not help you, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: That heart-seeking bow you wield... it is nothing but a tool for cowards, boy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: The Shield of Chaos... what a monstrous thing. How suitable for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You bear the Aegis so much like your uncle Zeus once did.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You think the Rail of Adamant can vanquish me? We'll momentarily find out.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You would take aim at me with Exagryph? Then, very well.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You would attack me with the savagery of the Twin Fists? Then, come.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Again, you seek to use the power of Malphon against me. Fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You are predictable in your arrivals here. Less so during the fight itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I was just thinking on when last I vanquished you. Come, refresh my memory.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: No matter how often you defeat me, I shall face you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Remember, I can always get here much, much faster than you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: No matter how quickly you're able to get here, I shall be waiting.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Again, you draw me from all my responsibilities back at the House.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I cannot let you go, boy. Some night such as this one, you'll understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I shall be interested to see if you can vanquish me again, here, boy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Come, show me everything you've learned of how to fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You've slipped by Cerberus again, I see. Then, draw your weapon, boy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I see it's time again that we begin our bloody business here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: This blasted place... how can you even stand it on the surface, here?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Who shall be first to return home this time, do you suppose?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: It seems it's time again I sent you home, myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Your rampage through my realm ends here and now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I thought you might arrive right about now. Then, let us have our fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You'll go not one step farther past this threshold, boy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: The world beyond my realm is not for you. You're going home.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I wonder, boy, who's going to kill whom this time around.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I cannot fathom why you wish to die repeatedly like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: So, Varatha and Gigaros are to clash again upon this empty field. Let us have at it, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I knew someday the Eternal Spear would turn on me. Let's see you wield it, boy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...Such contempt, for your own father. But we are not finished here, yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...Your youth provides you with a certain mindless strength. But, such power has its limits.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...Now that we had our little warm-up, it is time that we began in earnest.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...You strike at your own father. What choice does that leave me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...Hmph. All right, then, boy. You've stirred my anger suitably, by now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...Nary a whif of Mount Olympus on you, this time. How is it that you clambered all this way, without the generous support of your dear family? Doubtless you merely purged their blessings for some coin.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I resent that, Father! I'm more than capable of getting here all on my own, now, thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Such arrogance has led to many a mortal's downfall... and many a god's.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Odd. No stink of Olympus on you, this time. Have you truly come all this way without assistance from your relatives? Or were you overzealous at a Pool of Purging?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wouldn't you like to know? Not even Nyx believed that I could come this far without aid from the Olympians. Maybe I just have a knack for proving everybody wrong.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You have a knack for being struck down in various, ignoble ways, that much is certain. Come, then! Show me what you, yourself, can do!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: It is a testament to the awful might of Demeter that, even with the surge of heat created by the Pact of Punishment you toy with recklessly, this place remains a frozen waste.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: The Pact did make the road here a little more fraught than usual. But if it couldn't stop me, I can't imagine it could stop Demeter.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Forget her, and the blasted Pact. I'll stop you, myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...That pomegranate blossom... I caught its subtle scent as you approached. The Queen has her mother's strength. And much more self control.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, you're afraid of a little flower, now, Father? One that imbues me with the fiercest might of Olympus, granted, but still! It looks harmless, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I fear your little flower there much more than you. The Queen and her mother... they possess power over life. Thus, power over death.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Heh. Confronting you like this... it's different now. Still... it is a responsibility, just as before. There is to be no escape from my realm. Ultimately, I must see to that, myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's the spirit, Father! As for me, I find the candor of our interactions here to be rather refreshing, I must say. Especially the interactions between my weapons and your physique. You're tough, but you're an easy target.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: And you are scrawny, though slippery, blast you. All that pent-up rage behind your smiling words... come, unleash it once again. I expect you to go all out. To the death!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Already here? It seems our Underworld still has many failings I shall have to deal with, after this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Come show your father what you've learned of late. Do not hold back.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Would that you cleaned your chambers with the same devotion you show getting to these heights.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Untold murderous wretches in my realm, all at my beck and call, yet still you made it here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: You have circumvented all of my security measures again. But can you get through me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I trust you have retained enough of your strength through this ordeal to make this worth our while?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Catch your breath if necessary, and then let us see if you shall earn your bonus for this day.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Bah! Not one of my overseers managed to stop you, again? Must I do everything myself?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I've other work to get to! Arm yourself, and let us end this quickly, boy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: It seems our realm has many weaknesses. I shall deal with them shortly, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Let us see if the support you're getting from Olympus amounts to anything of use.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Blast, but you're persistent, even now! Though let us see how strong you are, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I should be getting back to my desk. But I suppose the work can wait.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Ah, there you are, boy! I was beginning to think I'd need a thicker cloak.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Well, Zagreus. Even if I vanquish you, arriving here is a sufficient feat.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I must admit, this is a welcome change of pace from all the parchmentwork.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I suppose that you must be eager to repay me for last time, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: I expect I shall be able to vanquish you again. Though, we shall see.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps the blasted Fates shall favor me, for once.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Our last exchange left much to be desired, for my part. But this time, we shall see.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: If you are here, then... the Olympians must still be lending you their aid. Just as before! I scarce believe the Queen's preposterous idea worked. That all it took to settle an old score was a blasted family feast.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Father, even you must have enjoyed that feast to some extent. We ought to do it more often! Good thing you trusted Mother's judgment on that one. So, then, to the death, once more?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Indeed. To the death, as always, boy. Zagreus. Ready yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: ...Tsch. To have been brought this low... by my own ungrateful child. I would expect such treatment from my brothers, but from you...? Nrrrgghhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Enjoying the delicate warmth of the surface, b-- Zagreus? Apparently, the generosity of the goddess of seasons knows no bounds.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Does feel a bit different, maybe. Though, Demeter said something about how this spot, in particular, would remain bereft of warmth, as a reminder. Do you think she knows what happened with Mother, really?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hades
dialogueLines.text: Demeter may harbor vengeful feelings and suspicions, but she is no fool. Experience teaches us to know when to quit. Though, evidently, that is a lesson you have yet to learn.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Huh. You mean to fight with me bare-handed this time, short one? I'm not ashamed to say the Champion himself defeated me in such a manner back when we drew breath.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wouldn't say the Twin Fists of Malphon counts as empty-handed, sir, though it surprises me to hear you could be beaten in such a way.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I was hungry and unprepared. Inexperienced. No longer. Come.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You must take pleasure in having to fight against me, short one. I, too, have come to look forward to it. Even if I end up falling to your strength.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I was going to say, I'd much prefer we settle this more amicably, couldn't we? Perhaps I could interest you in a fine bottle of Nectar? Ambrosia, even?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: No, thank you. These battles are enough. Besides... you won that Ambrosia from us, fair and square.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Well, you're back, short one. Or Prince. Whatever they call you. I'm not much for formality.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Not much for it either, here, Asterius. Though, speaking of which, how would you prefer to be addressed? The Minotaur, the Bull of Minos? Those aren't offensive to you, are they?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: They don't mean anything to me. They mean something to others. I have my name. You know it. Use it if you want. Now, enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You haven't spoken to your fist-weapons yet, have you, short one? 'I see you overcome the wild and make peace with death.' That's what you're supposed to tell them. I'm sure of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Just been a little tied up lately is all, but I will get to it, Asterius! I guess you'll want to know the result?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Yes. Here, I'll give you this chance to get home quickly, if you want to know, yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: There, on your hands... the weapons you call Malphon. They're just as I saw in my dream. Monstrous. Like me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Former property of someone called Gilgamesh. The beast-man he fought, Enkidu... you said they became friends? What said your dream about us on that front, sir?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: It didn't say much. Only that we would fight. Just as that Gilgamesh and Enkidu once did. Maybe the outcome will be the same, maybe it won't. We'll find out.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, you're from Crete, originally, aren't you, Asterius? I know this is a stretch, but... I don't suppose you ever heard of a commander by the name of Schelemeus? Captain of the Cretan second fleet, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: The king of Crete commanded the greatest navy in the world. Even I knew that, having never seen the light of day, the ships, the sea. Schelemeus, huh...? I might have heard the name. So what?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, Schelemeus was a real person, then? But, Skelly said... ah, you know what, it's nothing, Asterius. Shall we?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Yes. Let's.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Ah. I sense another furious battle here awaits us, short one. Although you're more inquisitive than usual...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Uhh, oh! It's just, I have an odd request. You see, the god of slumber, Hypnos? Colleague of mine. Huge fan. He's in desperate need of your autograph. Little personalized message? I don't suppose you'd be so kind. It'd mean a lot to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Autograph. I see. Then, it appears there are such gods as favor me. I will inscribe a bit of parchment for your colleague, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: The god-forsaken Minotaur impresses then upon a parchment a brief message to the god of sleep, and gives it to the favor-asking Prince.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Know, short one, that this favor is for you and not for Hypnos. Though he may challenge me himself, at any time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I wonder something, short one. You keep coming back. But I don't know why. You're trying to get out of here, aren't you? But something's killing you up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You've deduced more or less correctly, Asterius. And yes, let's say there are some challenges remaining once I get out of Elysium. Though I'd best say no more of it than that.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: That's fine. It's good to know you've fallen just as often as I have. I may not be the one to kill you. But I'll soften you up for whoever does.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You ever make it out of here, short one? I wonder, sometimes, what it must be like... the outside.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You've never been outside? Oh, right... the labyrinth thing. You lived in a horrible maze all your life. I haven't seen much of the surface, but... it's beautiful. Cold. When I'm there, though, I... feel out of place.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Out of place. I understand, I think. I don't see why you'd want to go somewhere like that. It can't live up to the stories. Why ruin it? Speaking of which...

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You bested the Champion, himself. Which means that I have little hope of vanquishing you, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So then, why bother fighting me? You don't have to do this. That Theseus is a highly questionable influence on you!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I see you plain, short one. I, too, was born of darkness. But, I chose the path of light. Even in death, it was not too late. <Snort> Not even for a monster, such as I. I trust your deaths will likewise be enlightening.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: We meet again, short one. For further battle.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Short one?! That's a low blow, sir. I thought you were the big-honorable type.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You mock my sense of honor. <Snort> Common tactic. Now prepare.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You choose to challenge me again. Not many in Elysium so dare. Then, I accept.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, I never challenged you! The entrance to your chamber looks like any other pathway in Elysium. I have no quarrel with you, sir!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: To step into my chamber is to challenge me. <Snort> It is a custom since the time I lived and breathed in darkness. I will send you into such a darkness now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: My next opponent has arrived in short order.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Short order? That was another jab about my height, wasn't it!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I do not jab. <Snort> All of my strength goes into every strike. Observe.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You have returned to my chamber. I trust you are familiar now with what this means.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It means I've challenged you to a battle of strength and glory, because apparently that's all there is to do for fun around here for those such as yourself?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: That... <Snort> Yes. Essentially that is correct. Prepare.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: It would seem <Snort> that the time for additional fun has arrived.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ah, yes, my favorite pastime: Getting butchered by a giant double-bladed axe. And sometimes trampled.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Then, good. You have much to enjoy in store.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Well, short one. It seems I must attempt to maim you here, so that the king and I might finally stop you at the gates.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, you hurt me with this 'short one' business almost as much as with that axe. Why isn't Theseus 'short one'? We're the same height!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: No. The king is greater. He is the greatest king of Athens! What are you? A willful son of a fickle god. I am beneath your father's notice. And you are beneath the king's. Now fight me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Observe this gleaming coat of bronze with which I am adorned, now, short one. <Snort> Your doing, as I'm to understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: My father is to thank for that, not me. I've proven difficult for him to contain, so now I'm beneficiary of his latest Pact of Punishment. You're welcome!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I hadn't thanked you, as of yet. Though, come. I'll repay you suitably for having me encased within this... suit.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Tell me, short one, where is your own armored suit? I would prefer not to do battle against one who's ill equipped.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, my father keeps the dress code very strict down there in Tartarus. No armor in the House! Never tried the stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Dress code. <Snort> I, too, follow a code. And it's a code that strictly states I cannot let you pass without a fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Even with this armor I was unable to best you one-to-one. Perhaps I lack as yet the necessary training to take full advantage of its benefit.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't suppose you have some good acquaintances with whom to train, until such time as you see fit to face me once again?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: No. <Snort> King Theseus is occupied with preparations of his own. You are my good acquaintance now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You come from the bowels of hell. This is not your place.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Agreed on both counts! Just passing through, so if you'll excuse me...

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I will not. <Snort> The Champion of Elysium would never back down from such a contest. Neither will I.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You come from the bowels of hell. This is not your place.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Agreed on both counts! Just passing through, so if you'll excuse me...

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I will not. <Snort> The Champion of Elysium would never back down from such a contest. Neither will I.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you're not the Champion of Elysium...? You're the Bull of Minos! I can't imagine who's even stronger than you. Is it Heracles? Tell me it's Heracles.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I will not. <Snort> You talk too much, short one. Come get destroyed.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Urgh... the might of Gilgamesh... I understand it now. But I won't make his friend's mistake. We'll fight again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Such strength... the strength of Gilgamesh. Thank you for the battle, short one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You are more skilled than I expected, short one. A re-match is most certainly in order.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're going to force a rematch each time I manage to defeat you, aren't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: We and our victories are all impermanent here in Elysium. We live, such as it is, for moments such as these.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I smell the blood on you, short one. <Snort> You are severely wounded. This will not be a fair fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: An unfair fight is not worth having, then, wouldn't you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I would not. An unfair fight is better than no fight at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You're hardly in a state to fight against me, here. Won't take too long to bring you death.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Great! Thanks for the encouragement, Asterius. Don't let me keep you waiting on that, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: If mere words can sap your will for battle, short one, then know this: You never stood a chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You look as though you withstood many foes in getting here this time. Barely withstood, that is.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: They took a lot out of me, yes. But you have no idea just how long and arduous a trip it is from Tartarus to here!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: What would you know of arduous? Try living in an ever-shifting maze of countless passageways. Now let me finish what your foes began.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You have met the Champion, the king. Let us see if you are fit to face him once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You mean face both of you, together. Which doesn't seem particularly fair.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Life isn't particularly fair, short one. Nor death. I'd have expected you to know as much. But here, have your fair fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Let us clash here again before you face the Champion. We never will allow you through without a fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're nothing like Theseus. Why go along with everything he says and does? You're your own man. And bull.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: King Theseus slew me in life, and saved me in death. I was cast into Erebus. A monster. He found me when he died. And used his influence to bring me here, with him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's nice of him and all, but sounds like you're even to me. Seeing as he killed you in the first place.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: No; we're not. And I will fight for him, and alongside him, for eternity. As recompense for helping me to understand exactly who I am.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Your challenge is hereby accepted, short one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Another challenger. <Snort> Come get destroyed.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I accept all challengers. No prior notice necessary.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You have returned. <Snort> I'm always ready to oppose you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You enter my chamber once again, short one. Then, good.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I expected you to reappear. <Snort> Let us fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Appears it's time we challenged one another once again. Prepare.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Good. <Snort> It feels as though I haven't crushed the life from anything in ages.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Returned to battle me for glory, short one? <Snort> Excellent.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Come fight me again, short one. We have eternity to grow stronger.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, short one. <Snort> Few in Elysium dare challenge me repeatedly.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: The short one from the bowels of hell has finally returned.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I heard you had again entered Elysium. <Snort> At last.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Hold nothing back against me, short one. Now, come.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Let us grow stronger in the ways of battle, short one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I always am prepared to fight whoever dares oppose me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Let us battle now before you face the Champion, himself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You seek the Champion. You must confront me, first.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Hrmm. <Snort> It has been long enough since my last fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I've been waiting for another round against you. Let's go. Now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: They said you were headed this way. <Snort> I said I'd stop you. Though, we'll see.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Each time we fight... I think I learn a little more. Let's start.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I had a feeling you would cross my path again. I've been looking forward to this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I'm going to run you down, or run you through. Your choice, short one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Told the king I'd try to stop you on the way. He thought I'd have no trouble. <Snort> We'll see.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Come on, short one. I've been waiting too long for this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You know what it takes to leave this chamber alive, short one. <Snort> Come on and fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I have my armor, short one. Let's see what you have.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Let's see if you can cut through my coat this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: This armor's heavy. But makes me even faster than before. <Snort> Watch.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You're quick, short one. Let's see if you can get past me again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Well, short one. I'm curious to see if you can best me once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: A worthy challenger returns. Let's see you defeat me again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Glory and honor to you if you can vanquish me again. Come!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I welcome the taste of defeat, short one. It is akin to feeling alive.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I'm still learning all your tricks, short one. Come show me more.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Let's see you get through me again, short one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I'm the one to challenge you this time, short one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I have looked forward to repaying you in kind for our last fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You're strong, short one. But I am just as obstinate as you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: The Champion awaits, short one. If you can get through me again, that is.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Good. Now I can get you back for last time. All I need is one clean hit.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You are resilient, short one, I will give you that.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I'm prepared to slaughter you again, short one. Come fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Interesting. <Snort> Most never challenge me again once I destroy them.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: What hope have you against the Champion, if you can't get through me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You have tenacity, short one. I have to give you that.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You do not take all your defeats to heart, do you? That's good.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Well fought. Though, your technique pales in comparison to that of the Champion of Elysium. I expect you'll soon see for yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I respect your strength, short one. The Champion of Elysium awaits. Until such time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You were a worthy challenger. Most welcome here. Until we meet again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You have earned your passage. I go now to recover for the next occasion that we meet.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for sparring with me, short one. I'm glad we've made a habit of it thus.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: This heat... it reminds me of when I was young. Living in darkness. Learning to fight. To kill.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I suppose you're about to put that learning to practice in another moment here?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I am. The heat fills me with strength. Let's see if it has any effect on you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I am returned once more to my more-earthly capability, short one. And I am able to move freely once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Fine change of pace, Asterius! Who needs that old, spectacular, custom-fitting suit of legendary armor, anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You mock me. Yet you know nothing of being trapped; enclosed in something and unable to break free. I need no armor to fight you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Everyone's saying that the Queen is back. Do you know what that means, short one?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, for me, at least, it happens to mean quite a bit. I'm her son!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: It doesn't mean a thing. We're still here to fight. Though, for what it's worth... my mother also was a queen. May yours treat you better than she treated me. Better than I'm about to treat you, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: You've come, short one. I dreamed of this very moment.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Asterius, we've fought how many times already? What's there to dream about?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: No. I dreamed. And in my dream, there was something I had to tell you. Rather... something you have to tell the weapons you wear on your hands. Tell them, 'I see you overcome the wild and make peace with death.'

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I know what that is, that's a waking-phrase. So I'm to speak it to Malphon. Wait, why are you helping me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: I don't know whether I'm helping you or not. But in my dream... an ancient hero fought a beast of a man, barehanded. They became friends, in the end, after a great battle. I long for a fight like that.

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I see you overcome the wild and make peace with death!

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: It is not often that my challengers return to face another slaughter at my hands.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, well. You're kind of in my way. What choice do I have?

dialogueLines.speaker: Minotaur
dialogueLines.text: Your choices led you here, and they will lead you to defeat, again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Skelly? Don't suppose you'd care to tell me how the infamous Twin Fists of Malphon came to be right here in this very courtyard, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Nope! Not any of my business, pal, which is to say, I didn't see a thing! And even if I did, it isn't what I'm paid to do! Don't you have someone in your fancy House there paid to keep watch over stuff like that? Ask them!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Pal, thanks again for that Nectar bottle from before, stuff just goes right through me like you won't believe! Though, I hope you're not going around giving that stuff away to every bozo you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, Skelly, you worried something bad is going to happen if I express a bit of generosity from time to time through our somewhat-illicit gift-giving custom around here?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: No, I'm worried you'll be wasting your time! If you give that stuff away, don't expect to get anything back. Well, not after the first time, anyway. Usually! I'm just saying... don't get your hopes up.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Maybe I just want to show thanks to my friends for its own sake!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Hey, just want to say, pal, that spooky crimson phoenix stuff that you were going on about before? I mean, whoa, you just say some nonsense and you get a brand-new spear? I was so impressed, I decided to try that trick for myself!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, gods... Skelly, what did you do...?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Relax, boyo, just had a little chat with your old serving platter over there after you left, is all. That thing hits pretty hard! So, then, I say to it, I see you through the eyes of the crimson phoenix... and you want to know what happened after that?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So you carelessly spoke secret words imparted by the Fates themselves to an infernal weapon forged at the dawn of creation, then what? Say, did you walk up to Aegis, or just shout over your shoulder?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Over my shoulder! Anyway, what happened next was nothing! Your Aegis kept on bobbing like a dummy. So then I went and said that mumbo jumbo to every other weapon, but nope! Doesn't work at all. So much for your fancy sorcery, huh?

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I miss my fancy sorcery already.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Skelly, how do you know all this stuff, by the way? I mean no offense, but you're a skeleton who just stands around. Yet you have this wealth of knowledge of the Underworld?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I know people, pal! We'll just have to leave it at that. Now let's see you break some of these bones, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, what exactly happened with the formerly pink window over there? Now it's got these sinister orange flames and an enormous Pact of Punishment hanging ominously over the exit. Father stop by?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Pal, I don't know a thing about it, honest! I was just minding my own business, when all the sudden, wham! I get hit right in the back. I fall to pieces, no idea what's going on! When I finally come to, the window job was done...!

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: As likely an explanation as any I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, mate? I picked up a painting for our lounge that looked a little like yourself. Reminds me... have you ever even been inside? Should come join us sometime.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Ah, you know, while I appreciate that, there, boyo... those kind of social spaces, they're not really for me. I kind of like it out here, just me, these bats whizzing around from time to time.... Besides! The old contract makes it pretty clear I got to stay right here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What if I picked you up bodily and brought you in? Contract have anything to say about that?

endLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
endLines.text: Yeah, no, that definitely wouldn't fly.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That Lucky Tooth you gave me, Skelly. First off, thanks again, and secondly... is this even yours? Had a run-in with Hermes, who got me thinking.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: What, haha, you going to believe everything that gabby guy tells you, now, boyo? He doesn't know a thing about me!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Never said he did. Though since you let on... what's your relationship to him? Working for him?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: No! Nope. Definitely not working for him, closer to a peer relationship. Well, other than the fact that he's a god and I'm just... well, what you see is what you get with me, pal. Hey, you won't tell anyone, will you? I'm just here to help, I swear!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: All right, fess up, Skelly, who are you working for? I know it's not Achilles. It's not Nyx. Dusa has no idea who you are. My father... no way. Hypnos? Give me a break. So the House is ruled out.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Sorry, pal, my lips are sealed. How about we change the subject?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How about you tell me who your sources are?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Nope!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Tell me.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: No way.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Tell me!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: I said no!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Tell me, Skelly.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Nuh-uh.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, tell me, please?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: No. Means. No.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why won't you tell me?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Nice try, pal.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What if I never hit you again?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So help me, Skelly, if you don't tell me who you're working for, there will be no more violence between us, ever.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: No... please, have mercy, boyo...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No mercy, Skelly. Tell me. Or else... it will be only pleasantries between us from here on out.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Nrrrghh, all right! But you got to promise me something, promise not to let on or tell anybody in your fancy House, all right? Or else I'm done for. I'm serious!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I promise. So...?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: ...It's Charon.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon! Huh.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. He's rich. He's got connections to guys like me, and I think he's got a horse in this chariot race you've got going on around here with Papa.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What's his angle?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: No idea. Maybe he likes you, maybe he's bored.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And Hermes... he's working for Charon, too?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Yep! Now, let's pretend like none of this ever happened, so I can stick around, all right? Remember, pal, you promised!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Skelly, did you tell Charon and Hermes about our little conversation from before, about your sources? Because, I didn't tell them, but... they seemed to know.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Boyo, thought we agreed we weren't going to be having conversations on the subject from here on. Spares me having to spill my guts with my sources later.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I didn't get you into too much trouble, did I?

endLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
endLines.text: If you did, pal, I would not be here right now!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Skelly, what with everything out in the open now, with the Queen and Charon, and all that... mind if I mention some of this to Thanatos? Charon's his brother, after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Yeah, now that you mention it, I guess that part of the old contract no longer applies. So, go right ahead! Appreciate you asking, pal.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Skelly? You look like you know your way around Tartarus. Found some of this ancient Titan blood out there, and I was looking to score some more.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Ah, you've come to the right place regarding that, there, pal! It's your mean weapons back there that can find the stuff. Once you get all the way through Tartarus with one, that's all the blood you're going to get! For the time being anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How am I supposed to keep track of which weapons I've earned these with, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: I don't know, there's always the invention of writing stuff down? You'll figure something out.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Skelly, you mentioned the ancient bit of blood I found in Tartarus. Know what I'm supposed to do with this stuff?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Wouldn't know a thing about it! Definitely doesn't have anything to do with those Infernal Arms of yours, some of which appear to still be unavailable to you, as I understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: So the strangest thing happened while you were out, boyo! One of your weapon holder things back there, it picked up this eerie glow! Could see it from the corner of my eye!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Calm down, Skelly. I'm sure whatever it is is perfectly normal for a legendary semi-sentient Titan-slaying weapon from the dawn of time.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: That's just it! Those weapons of yours, they have seen some real business, pal. You're giving them a real workout, so maybe they're starting to like you or something? Loosening up a bit, you know? Like you and me!

endLines.speaker: CharProtag
endLines.text: I should go check it out.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: You know, pal, when you're not around and it's just me and all your weapons, I mean your Infernal Arms right over there, between us they are making me a bit uncomfortable.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Can't blame you, mate. I wouldn't want to be stuck in a chamber with a bunch of legendary, quite possibly malevolent killing artifacts. Anything I can do?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Oh I didn't mean it like a bad thing, boyo! The weirder you make those things, the more it gives me the shakes, and keeps me hopping like this nice and steady till the next time you get back! So, thanks!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, mate, won't you say who hired you? Was it Achilles? Couldn't have been Nyx...? Is it that big a deal?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: I am an individual of some integrity, boyo, and protect my sources with my life! You'll have to kill me if you want to make me squeal!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, uh, Skelly? How'd you get here, anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know, just kind of zapped right in, like Papa lets us do! Some of us sometimes. Why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I meant... how did you get to the realm of the dead, in the first place. Lord Hermes whisk you to the entryway? Thanatos scoop you up, Charon give you a lift, what?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Oh, haha! Well, that's a little personal, don't you think, boyo? I... I don't like to talk about it much. So, if I could have a little privacy...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Skelly, is it really true you don't want to talk about how you got here? Just curious who you used to be. What you were like. Your real name.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Hey, what makes you think this isn't my real name, pal? I'm offended!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sorry, what I meant was... I was just interested to get to know you better, after all you've done to help. Anything you can share? Trade you for a detail about me!

dialogueLines.speaker: SkellyBackstory
dialogueLines.text: ...Well all right... I was once a commander of men. Name of Schelemeus. Captain of the Cretan second fleet, sailing on Athens under King Minos. We were unstoppable... but then one day... one wrong turn... and we sailed straight into the waters of Charybdis.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Skelly, that's... that's incredible! So then... you and your crew, you perished at sea?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: I, uh, pfff, hahaha, boyo, you are something else, you know? All that Schelemeus stuff, I could have had you eating out of my hand for days!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I get it. You got me. Fine, then, Schelemeus, but I don't appreciate the trick.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Skelly... I'm serious. You really won't tell me anything about you? You're just going to leave me to speculate, forever?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Skelly... or should I say Schelemeus? I happened to have a chat with somebody familiar with that name. Large bull-headed fellow originally from Crete. You said you made it all up! What's going on?

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Never said I made it up, pal! I just knew you weren't going to believe me, I mean, look at me! I look like a captain of the Cretan second fleet to you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't know. I've never met one. Do they all just stand around doing nothing?

dialogueLines.speaker: SkellyBackstory
dialogueLines.text: Well, sounds like somebody's never had to lead like fifty-or-something sailors in a voyage across the sea in a Minoan galley with nothing but the stars to guide 'em! What do you think the captains do, pull the oars?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what about Hermes? He gave me the impression you might have been somebody else. Nobody in particular. Not some great sea-captain.

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: What is it with you gods talking behind the backs of all your friends, boyo?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't change the subject!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Don't change the subject!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Less yakking, more smacking, boyo, now come on!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Less chatter, more batter, boyo, hit me already!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Less talking, more socking, here, pal!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
dialogueLines.text: Come on, boyo, less gabbing, more stabbing over here!

dialogueLines.speaker: TrainingMelee
